stories.title: Trump Administration Wants to Make It More Difficult to Evade a Military Draft
stories.description:
With automatic Selective Service registration, it would be harder for Americans to dodge a potential military draft. 
 The post  Trump Administration Wants to Make It More Difficult to Evade a Military Draft  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: We Need You: Our Privacy Cannot Afford a Clean Extension of Section 702
stories.description: We go through this every couple of years: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA), which   of Americans’ communications with foreign persons overseas   is up for renewal. As always, Congress can reauthorize it with or without changes, or just let it expire. We know, we k

stories.title: Meta’s New AI Asked for My Raw Health Data—and Gave Me Terrible Advice
stories.description: Meta’s Muse Spark model offers to analyze users’ health data, including lab results. Beyond the obvious privacy risks, it’s not a capable stand-in for a real doctor.

stories.title: A Redditor Criticized ICE. Trump Is Trying to Unmask Them by Dragging the Company to a Secret Grand Jury.
stories.description:
An ICE summons to get the user’s identity failed. Advocates worry the move to a grand jury signals an escalation of the war on dissent. 
 The post  A Redditor Criticized ICE. Trump Is Trying to Unmask Them by Dragging the Company to a Secret Grand Jury.  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Yikes, Encryption’s Y2K Moment is Coming Years Early
stories.description: Google    moved up its estimated deadline    for quantum preparedness in cryptography to 2029—only 33 months from now. That’s earlier than    previous     deadlines   , and they proposed the new post-quantum migration deadline because of two    new     papers    that comprise a big jump in the

stories.title: Meta Cafeteria Workers Did What Execs Won’t: Took on ICE and Won
stories.description: In Seattle, activists inside tech giants are leaning into grassroots fundraising and peer support as petitions and protests draw little response from executives.

stories.title: This AI Wearable From Ex-Apple Engineers Looks Like an iPod Shuffle
stories.description: Two former Apple Vision Pro developers made an AI wearable that only listens when you tap it. They hope to win where other AI gadgets have fumbled: privacy.

stories.title: Conflicting Rulings Leave Anthropic in ‘Supply-Chain Risk’ Limbo
stories.description: A US appeals court ruling is at odds with a separate, lower court decision from March, leaving uncertainty about if and how the US military can use the AI company's Claude model.

stories.title: How our digital devices are putting our right to privacy at risk
stories.description: Law professor Andrew Guthrie Ferguson chats with Ars about his new book,  Your Data Will Be Used Against You .

stories.title: We Called Out the Pentagon for Undercounting U.S. Casualties in Iran. They Keep Doing It.
stories.description:
After we exposed what one source called a “casualty cover-up,” the Pentagon offered another lowball count. 
 The post  We Called Out the Pentagon for Undercounting U.S. Casualties in Iran. They Keep Doing It.  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: The US Army Is Building Its Own Chatbot for Combat
stories.description: The AI system, trained on real military data, is meant to give soldiers mission-critical information.

stories.title: 👁 Selling Mass Surveillance | EFFector 38.7
stories.description: Time and time again, we've seen police surveillance suffer from 'mission creep'—technology sold as a way to prevent heinous crimes ends up enforcing traffic violations, tracking protestors, and more. In  our latest EFFector newsletter , we're diving into this troubling pattern and sharing all th

stories.title: Podcast: Wildlife Cops Are Searching AI Cameras for ICE
stories.description: How Florida conservation police are tapping into Flock for ICE; Wikipedia's AI ban; and how the app TeleGuard uploads users' private keys.

stories.title: Microsoft Abruptly Terminates VeraCrypt Account, Halting Windows Updates
stories.description: Updates to VeraCrypt, a popular and long-running piece of encryption, are now thrown into doubt because of a seemingly unexplained Microsoft decision.

stories.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
stories.description: End-of-life routers in homes and small offices hacked in 120 countries.

stories.title: Digital Hopes, Real Power: How the Arab Spring Fueled a Global Surveillance Boom
stories.description:
This is the third installment of a blog series reflecting on the global digital legacy of the 2011 Arab uprisings. You can read the first post  here , and the second  here .   
  When people recall the 2011 uprisings across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA), they often picture crowded sq

stories.title: With Trump Threatening Genocide in Iran, Military Must Disobey His Orders, Former Pentagon Officials Say
stories.description:
“What President Trump is describing as the destruction of ‘a whole civilization’ would be a war crime, plain and simple.” 
 The post  With Trump Threatening Genocide in Iran, Military Must Disobey His Orders, Former Pentagon Officials Say  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Row over ‘virtual gated community’ AI surveillance plan in Toronto neighbourhood
stories.description: Rosedale residents considering car licence plate-scanning Flock system in bid to tackle property crime  A row has broken out in one of Canada’s wealthiest neighbourhoods over plans to use an AI-powered surveillance system to create the country’s first “virtual gated community” to combat surging pro

stories.title: Border Patrol Agents Sold Challenge Coins With ‘Charlotte’s Web’ Characters in Riot Gear
stories.description: Nonprofits run out of US Border Patrol stations are also selling other “operation”-themed coins that include a phrase popularized by the Proud Boys, potentially in violation of government rules.

stories.title: Wildlife Conservation Police Are Searching Thousands of Flock Cameras for ICE
stories.description: Ron DeSantis has empowered hundreds of Florida conservation police to work directly with ICE.

stories.title: Maytag Promo Codes and Deals: Appliances Under $300
stories.description: Upgrade your home for less with these verified Maytag discount codes, military savings, and limited-time closeout offers on washers, dryers, and more.

stories.title: Ice Age dice show early Native Americans may have understood probability
stories.description: Ice Age hunter-gatherer "were intentionally relying on random outcomes in repeatable, rule-based ways."

stories.title: The FAA’s “Temporary” Flight Restriction for Drones is a Blatant Attempt to Criminalize Filming ICE
stories.description:
Legal intern Raj Gambhir was the principal author of this post.  
 The Trump administration has restricted the First Amendment right to record law enforcement by issuing an unprecedented  nationwide flight restriction  preventing private drone operators, including professional and citizen journ

stories.title: Tech Nonprofits to Feds: Don’t Weaponize Procurement to Undermine AI Trust and Safety
stories.description: While the very public fight continues  between the Department of Defense and Anthropic  over whether the government can punish a company for refusing to allow its technology to be used for mass surveillance, another agency of the U.S. government is quietly working to ensure that this dispute wil

stories.title: Iran Shoots Down F-15 Fighter Jet After Trump Bragged They Had No Capability
stories.description:
“We are unstoppable as a military force," Trump boasted before Iran shot down one U.S. plane and another crashed near the Strait of Hormuz. 
 The post  Iran Shoots Down F-15 Fighter Jet After Trump Bragged They Had No Capability  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: CBP Facility Codes Sure Seem to Have Leaked Via Online Flashcards
stories.description: The Quizlet flashcards, which WIRED found through basic Google searches, seem to include sensitive information about gate security at Customs and Border Protection locations.

stories.title: Google and Amazon: Acknowledged Risks, And Ignored Responsibilities
stories.description: In late 2024, we urged  Google and Amazon  to honor their human rights commitments, to be more transparent with the public, and to take meaningful action to address the risks posed by Project Nimbus, their cloud computing contract that includes Israel’s Ministry of Defense and the Israeli Securi

stories.title: Artemis II Astronauts Have ‘Two Microsoft Outlooks’ and Neither Work
stories.description: In space, no one can hear you scream at Microsoft’s legacy software.

stories.title: Unmasking the Paramilitary Agents Behind Trump’s Violent Immigration Crackdown
stories.description: A WIRED analysis of DHS records identified dozens of specialized federal agents who used force against US civilians during the largest known deployment of its kind in US history.

stories.title: “Casualty Cover-Up”: The Pentagon Is Hiding U.S. Losses Under Trump in the Middle East
stories.description:
The Pentagon has sent outdated statements on the number of U.S. troops killed or wounded during the Iran war, resulting in undercounts. 
 The post  “Casualty Cover-Up”: The Pentagon Is Hiding U.S. Losses Under Trump in the Middle East  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: NASA is leading the way to the Moon, but the military won't be far behind
stories.description: "I just don't want to get caught flat-footed when we start to have to protect US interests out there."

stories.title: weekly Update 03/31
stories.description: This past week, on Tuesday 3/24 we went to the Board of Police Commissioner’s meeting, and we held our monthly Data Driven Policing meeting where we had lots and lots of updates about Flock and how we are continuing our fight against it. Later that week, on Wednesday, we did community watch a

stories.title: The US Military’s GPS Software Is an $8 Billion Mess
stories.description: The GPS Next-Generation Operational Control System was due for completion in 2016. Ten years later, the software for controlling the military’s GPS satellites still doesn’t work.

stories.title: Opposing ICE Might Save the Country. It Could Also Ruin Your Life
stories.description: For months, lone vibe coder Rafael Concepcion has obsessively built tools to counter the federal immigration crackdown—pivoting as he’s been outmatched. He’s also lost his job and become a target.

stories.title: Two Thirds of People Arrested by ICE in Minnesota Surge Had No Criminal Records, New Data Reveals
stories.description:
The White House had said all the thousands of people arrested were “dangerous criminal” immigrants. 
 The post  Two Thirds of People Arrested by ICE in Minnesota Surge Had No Criminal Records, New Data Reveals  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: What Would We All Say If Iran Razed MIT Because of Military-Related Research?
stories.description:
The destruction of parts of two universities in Iran fits with Israel’s M.O. of crippling countries’ ability to rebuild. 
 The post  What Would We All Say If Iran Razed MIT Because of Military-Related Research?  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Trump’s Secret Wars on the World Keep Expanding
stories.description:
An analysis by The Intercept reveals that the “peace” president has embroiled the U.S. in more than 20 military interventions, armed conflicts, and wars. 
 The post  Trump’s Secret Wars on the World Keep Expanding  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: After 16 years and $8 billion, the military's new GPS software still doesn't work
stories.description: "It's a very stressing program. We are still considering how to ensure we move forward."

stories.title: EFF's Cindy Cohn on The Daily Show! Tonight Monday, March 30
stories.description: EFF Executive Director Cindy Cohn will be on The Daily Show tonight, Monday March 30, at 11 pm ET and PT, speaking with host Jon Stewart. Cindy will discuss her long history of fighting for privacy online and her new book,    Privacy’s Defender: My Thirty-Year Fight Against Digital Surveillance

stories.title: ‘They feel true’: political deepfakes are growing in influence – even if people know they aren’t real
stories.description: AI images of people – such as women in military contexts – are making money and serving as propaganda, researchers say  Online content creators are not just building fake images and videos of prominent public figures, they are also fabricating people and using them in military contexts, which can m

stories.title: Acer Promo Codes and Deals: Save 40% on Bundles
stories.description: From 40% off accessory bundles to verified student and military discounts, here's how to save on Predator, Nitro, and Swift laptops and monitors at Acer.com.

stories.title: ICE Agents Frustrate Airport Workers as Shutdown Drags On
stories.description: “ICE are here and they’re doing literally nothing to help,” said an airline worker in New York.

stories.title: How One Playwright is Using Theatre to Expose the Surveillance State
stories.description: As the ACLU fights to protect people’s privacy, playwright Matthew Libby discusses his play about the private companies fueling the government’s surveillance of immigrants.

stories.title: Police Used Flock to Give a Man a Traffic Ticket
stories.description: “CAPTURED ON FLOCK CAMERA 31 MM 1 HOLDING PHONE IN LEFT HAND.”

stories.title: Drones For People Not Just Police and Corporations
stories.description: ACLU white paper warns the technology may serve everyone but ordinary people

stories.title: Using a VPN May Subject You to NSA Spying
stories.description: US lawmakers are pressing Tulsi Gabbard to reveal whether using a VPN can strip Americans of their constitutional protections against warrantless surveillance.

stories.title: Why ICE Is Allowed to Impersonate Law Enforcement
stories.description: “There's no accountability,” one expert tells WIRED of ICE’s ability to lie to the public. "The consequence of this is that it’s going to be a systemic harm across all law enforcement.”

stories.title: Pentagon Wants It to Be Illegal for Reporters to Ask “Unauthorized” Questions
stories.description:
The Trump admin wants to criminalize a key part of journalists doing their jobs — a broadside attack on a free press. 
 The post  Pentagon Wants It to Be Illegal for Reporters to Ask “Unauthorized” Questions  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Apple begins age checks in the UK with latest iOS update
stories.description: Move follows government pressure on smartphone makers to do more to protect children online.

stories.title: How to Keep ICE Agents Out of Your Devices at Airports
stories.description:
If you’re traveling, follow these digital security practices to keep federal authorities from getting into your phone. 
 The post  How to Keep ICE Agents Out of Your Devices at Airports  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: How American Camouflage Conquered the World
stories.description: The world-famous MultiCam pattern was designed for the military by two Brooklyn hipsters. Now everyone—from babies to ICE agents—is suited up for battle.

stories.title: Electronic Frontier Foundation to swap leaders as AI, ICE fights escalate
stories.description: Public interest in government tech abuses is peaking. EFF's new leader plans to build on that.

stories.title: Kalshi and Polymarket ban insider trading as senators look to curb prediction markets
stories.description: Top prediction market sites usher in new guardrails after senators introduced bill that could limit booming industry   Kalshi  and  Polymarket , the two biggest prediction market sites, rushed to institute new industry guardrails and add new surveillance tools on Monday after two key senators  anno

stories.title: A Top Google Search Result for Claude Plugins Was Planted by Hackers
stories.description: Hackers paid to make a malicious link the top Google Search result.

stories.title: Your Body Is Betraying Your Right to Privacy
stories.description: Attachment to smart devices and biometric surveillance leaves Americans more vulnerable to police searches than ever. Left unchecked it will only get worse.

stories.title: ‘Get Down! Get Down! They’re Gonna See Us!’: Six Months of Hiding From ICE
stories.description: A family in Chicago has been terrified to leave their apartment. Agents could be anywhere.

stories.title: ICE Paid the Salaries of This Town’s Entire Police Force
stories.description: Under a Homeland Security program, police departments around the US are signing up to assist in immigration enforcement. The cops of Carroll, New Hampshire, are going all in—and they’re likely not alone.

stories.title: A Mysterious Numbers Station Is Broadcasting Through the Iran War
stories.description: First heard as US and Israeli strikes on Iran began, the shortwave broadcast has since been traced to a US military base in Germany—but its purpose and its operator remain unclear.

stories.title: Weekly Update 03/23
stories.description: This week, we visited Cal State LA to chat with students about art in the movement, as well as Flock, and our work to fight against the war on youth. On Tuesday 3/17, we attended the Board of Police Commissioner’s Meeting which was later canceled! We had our monthly General Community Meeting

stories.title: ICE Invades Airports Across the US
stories.description: Agents from ICE are being deployed to over a dozen airports around the country, including New York, Atlanta, and Chicago.

stories.title: Pentagon Reveals Attacks in Latin America Are Just the Beginning
stories.description:
With “Operation Total Extermination” and Trump’s threats against Cuba, expect more U.S. military strikes in the region. 
 The post  Pentagon Reveals Attacks in Latin America Are Just the Beginning  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: More Than 1 Million People in Lebanon Have Been Displaced. These Are Their Stories.
stories.description:
Israel’s military strikes in Lebanon sparked a humanitarian crisis, driving thousands to the streets of Beirut. 
 The post  More Than 1 Million People in Lebanon Have Been Displaced. These Are Their Stories.  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.description: Anthropic fought against the government’s misuse of its technology, but authorities are buying Americans’ data, enabling them to surveil citizens at scale  The FBI declares it can conduct mass surveillance without AI, despite Anthropic’s protest.  A central part of the standoff between Anthropic an

stories.title: Hegseth Makes Troops Prove “Sincerely Held” Faith in Latest Beard Crackdown
stories.description:
Hegseth’s obsession with beards risks jeopardizing religious liberties as the military undergoes an apparent Christian nationalist turn. 
 The post  Hegseth Makes Troops Prove “Sincerely Held” Faith in Latest Beard Crackdown  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Congress Is Dropping the Ball with a Clean Extension of FISA
stories.description: Two years ago, Congress passed the “Reforming Intelligence and Securing America” Act (RISAA) that included nominal reforms to    Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act (FISA)   . The bill unfortunately included some problematic expansions of the law  —  but it also included a

stories.title: Once again, ULA can't deliver when the US military needs a satellite in orbit
stories.description: ULA's Vulcan launch vehicle is grounded after a solid rocket booster anomaly last month.

stories.title: A Top Democrat Is Urging Colleagues to Support Trump’s Spy Machine
stories.description: Congressman Jim Himes claims a sweeping surveillance authority should stay intact because he hasn't seen abuses by Kash Patel's FBI, according to internal messaging obtained by WIRED.

stories.title: Pentagon Implores Civilian Workers to Join ICE “Volunteer Force”
stories.description:
The Defense Department is recruiting on behalf of DHS while the latter faces public backlash and a continued lack of government funding.

stories.title: The Danger Behind Meta Killing End-to-End Encryption for Instagram DMs
stories.description: Meta blamed users for not opting into the privacy-protecting feature. Experts fear the move could be the first major domino to fall for end-to-end encryption tech worldwide.

stories.title: Data Centers Are Military Targets Now
stories.description:
With militaries increasingly relying on artificial intelligence, data centers have emerged as new targets for strikes. 
 The post  Data Centers Are Military Targets Now  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: Pentagon Claims It Needs Additional $200 Billion to Pay for War on Iran
stories.description:
“Obviously, it takes money to kill bad guys,” said Pete Hegseth, when asked about the funding request.

stories.description: OpenAI plans to allow sexting with ChatGPT. A human-AI interaction expert warns of a privacy nightmare.

stories.title: Mapping Google's Unmappable City
stories.description: How filmmaker Chris Parr put North Oaks, Minnesota on the map.

stories.title: Air Force Academy Prepares Ideological Overhaul, With Erika Kirk Bringing “Bold Christian Faith”

stories.title: Confessions of the ICE Agent Whisperer
stories.description: Federal immigration enforcement agents usually won't talk to the media—but they will talk to independent journalist Karl Loftus.

stories.title: Users hate it, but age-check tech is coming. Here's how it works.
stories.description: On-device face scans and cross-platform age keys decrease privacy risks, but trust issues abound.

stories.title: Justice Department Says Anthropic Can’t Be Trusted With Warfighting Systems
stories.description: In response to Anthropic’s lawsuit, the government said it lawfully penalized the company for trying to limit how its Claude AI models could be used by the military.

stories.title: 'I Have No Rights': Minnesota Residents Sue Trump Administration for Racial Profiling, Stops Without Suspicion, and Warrantless Arrests
stories.description: Immigration arrests have upended life in Minnesota as citizens detail unlawful and violent interactions with ICE in court testimonies as part of an ACLU lawsuit against the Trump administration.

stories.title: Why We Have to Fight Back Against ICE Protesters’ Terror Convictions
stories.description:
Since his first inauguration, Trump has been throwing charges at protesters and seeing what sticks. He always failed — until now. 
 The post  Why We Have to Fight Back Against ICE Protesters’ Terror Convictions  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.title: These aren’t AI firms, they’re defense contractors. We can’t let them hide behind their models
stories.description: From Gaza to Iran, the pattern is the same: precision weapons, chosen blindness, and dead children. The cost of failing to regulate AI warfare is already too high  There is an Israeli military strategy called the “fog procedure”. First used during the second intifada, it’s an unofficial rule that r

A row of customer service desks, divided by plastic screens, with landline phones, promotional leaflets and staff bu

stories.title: Anti-ICE Protesters Convicted on Terrorism Charges for Wearing All Black
stories.description:
The government won on most of its charges, including convicting defendants for moving a box of radical zines. 
 The post  Anti-ICE Protesters Convicted on Terrorism Charges for Wearing All Black  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.description: Less than a decade ago, Google employees scuttled any military use of its AI. stories.title: Palantir Demos Show How the Military Could Use AI Chatbots to Generate War Plans
stories.description: Software demos and Pentagon records detail how chatbots like Anthropic’s Claude could help the Pentagon analyze intelligence and suggest next steps.

stories.title: ‘Invasive’ AI-led mass surveillance in Africa violating freedoms, warn experts
stories.description: Countries across the continent have spent more than $2bn on Chinese tracking technology that is not ‘necessary or proportionate’, new report finds  The rapid expansion of AI-powered mass-surveillance systems across Africa is violating citizens’ right to privacy and having a chilling effect on socie

stories.title: Binance sues WSJ, panicked by gov’t probes into sanctioned crypto transfers
stories.description: Binance’s lawsuit accusing WSJ of defamation unlikely to stall government probes.

stories.description:

stories.title: From Flock to ICE, Here’s a Breakdown of How You’re Being Watched
stories.description: To better understand what exactly we’re looking at in this dystopian surveillance hellscape, 404 Media’s Jason Koebler and Joseph Cox joined Reddit's r/technology for an Ask Me Anything session.

stories.title: Amazon is determined to use AI for everything – even when it slows down work
stories.description: Corporate employees said Amazon’s race to roll out AI is leading to surveillance, slop and ‘more work for everyone’. stories.title: Anduril snaps up space surveillance firm ExoAnalytic Solutions
stories.description: The deal could give Anduril a better shot at lucrative missile defense contracts through the "Golden Dome" system.

stories.title: The Government Must Not Force Companies to Participate in AI-powered Surveillance
stories.description: The rapidly escalating conflict between Anthropic and the Pentagon, which started when the company  refused  to let the government use its technology to spy on Americans, has now gone to court. The Department of Defense retaliated by designating the company a “supply chain risk” (SCR). Now, Anth

stories.title: Scientists Discover Vast Ancient Trade Network That Rewrites History with Parrot DNA
stories.description: “I think we often underestimate their capabilities,” said one of the researchers who uncovered  a pre-Inca trade route linking the Amazon rainforest to the Pacific coast.

stories.title: Anthropic sues US over blacklisting; White House calls firm "radical left, woke"
stories.description: Anthropic says it was blacklisted for opposing autonomous weapons, mass surveillance.

stories.title: DHS Ousts CBP Privacy Officers Who Questioned ‘Illegal’ Orders
stories.description: Department of Homeland Security leaders removed top privacy officers who objected to mislabeling government records to block their public release, WIRED has learned.

stories.title: US military contractor likely built iPhone hacking tools used by Russian spies in Ukraine
stories.description: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor said some of those hacking tools were theirs.

stories.title: With World's Eyes on Iran, Israel Locks Down the West Bank
stories.description:
The Israeli military has closed checkpoints around the West Bank, restricting Palestinians’ movement as settler violence ramps up. 
 The post  With World’s Eyes on Iran, Israel Locks Down the West Bank  appeared first on  The Intercept .

stories.description: Google found a series of hacking tools they said were used by a Russian espionage group and a cybercriminal group in China. Sources from a U.S. government defense contractor said some of those hacking tools were theirs.
